sanitization.policy: fail-closed publishable transcript: hidden reasoning and tool payloads are excluded; credentials, tokens, SSH material, IP addresses, private local paths, environment values, and sensitive infrastructure details are redacted or omitted.
sanitization.uncertainty: medium; cloud commands and provider status contained infrastructure identifiers, so this checkpoint records sanitized scientific and operational facts only

ledger: Candidate two, cumulative_256_256, completed the 8-epoch short stage for COBA and PING with one live pod at a time and separate collector pods.
COBA selected epoch 5 with validation accuracy 32.84313725490196%, validation cross-entropy 2.159944314223069, selected E rate 71.34302236519608 Hz, no skipped steps, and no non-finite batches.

PING selected epoch 7 with validation accuracy 42.76960784313726%, validation cross-entropy 1.8651616527484014, selected E/I rates 11.079091988357844 / 39.40600107230392 Hz, no skipped steps, and no non-finite batches.
Matched validation curves, activity curves, and input/E/I rasters for candidate two were published under artifacts/data/exp071 while preserving the candidate-one artifact record.
Candidate two is finite, active, and clean, but it underperforms candidate one on both cells: candidate one selected COBA 60.049019607843135% and PING 57.96568627450981%.
The registered short-screen promotion target is therefore candidate one, cumulative_baseline, not the two-hidden-layer candidate.
The active pod list was empty after both candidate-two collections.
Exact provider billing had not reconciled at checkpoint time. The temporary cumulative timestamp-based estimate is 1.76 USD total, well below the 40 USD ceiling.
Pending work is to run the registered 40-epoch promotion for candidate one, COBA and PING one pod at a time, unless stopped by the scientist.

messages.content: COBA candidate-two artifacts are collected and the collector reaped; active pod list is empty. I’m validating the collected COBA result before deciding whether to spend on PING for this candidate.

COBA candidate two completed cleanly but weakly: best epoch 5 at 32.84% validation, E active around 71 Hz, no skipped/non-finite updates. Because the registered candidate comparison is matched COBA/PING, I’m still running PING candidate two before making the promotion decision; otherwise we’d be peeking on one cell and stopping asymmetrically.
PING is healthy and actually a bit faster: epoch 2/8 is at 26.84% validation, E/I active, clean. ETA for PING completion is roughly 14–16 minutes.
PING epoch 5/8 has reached 41.05%, clean and active. Better than COBA candidate two, still below candidate-one PING’s 57.97%. Three epochs to go.
PING self-terminated and the active pod list is empty. Collecting candidate-two artifacts now.
PING candidate two is collected and collector reaped; active pod list empty. I’m validating both candidate-two cells and then I’ll publish the short result into exp071.
Candidate two is published locally. I’m making the report compare both short candidates directly so the promotion decision is explicit rather than buried in raw JSON.
